Name: name
circuits: Circuits for sharp, sharpf inside and notifications
donat: I'm not a professional developer and this is a private project, the development takes place in my spare time, if you want to support this you can thank me with a donation under PayPal. You are also welcome to send me feature requests for future versions of the adapter.
info_words: You can edit the notifications at words tab
input_logs: You can edit the notifications at words tab
opt_night: Control configured states during night rest
opt_night_silent: no speech output during night rest
opt_say_night: Warnings at the beginning of the night's rest
opt_say_warn: Warnings at the beginning of the warn circuit activate
opt_siren: activate outside siren at burgle sharp inside
please: Please, do not use the same states like the main states
second: All time inputs are in second
sendTo_Chips: Notification instnces like Email, Pushover or Telegram
send_activation_warnings_night: notification when warnings at beginning night rest
send_notification_changes: send notifications during sharp, scharp inside and night rest
silent_flash: flash light frequenz at silent alarm
text_changes: 5 - Phrase at changes sharp and sharp inside
text_changes_night: 9 - Phrase at changes during night rest
text_one: one - Phrase for additional alarm type one (Fire)
text_two: two - Phrase for additional alarm type two (Water)
text_warning: 4 - Phrase of warnings at the beginning night rest and sharp inside
desc_trigger_activation_failed: Activation failed due to open circuits
desc_trigger_mode_any: Trigger on every update, even same value
desc_trigger_mode_ne: Trigger only when value actually changes
desc_use_enable_with_delay: Arm the alarm system with countdown delay
info_general: Configure the main alarm settings: activation delays, siren durations, night rest schedule, password protection, and general behavior options for the alarm system.
info_notifications: Configure notification instances (Email, Pushover, Telegram) and select which alarm events should trigger notifications. Customize notification texts in the Words tab.
info_states: Define the monitoring circuits — the sensors and states that the alarm system monitors. Assign each circuit to alarm modes: sharp (full protection), sharp inside (perimeter), silent alarm, and notifications.
info_sayit: Configure text-to-speech instances for voice announcements. Define which alarm events trigger speech output and customize the announcement phrases below.
info_shortcuts: Output shortcuts map alarm states to external actuators (e.g., lights, sirens). Input shortcuts allow external switches or buttons to control the alarm system. Important: avoid creating loops!
info_words_tab: Customize the log messages and notification texts for each alarm event. These texts appear in the ioBroker log and are used for notification messages.
info_other_alarms: Configure up to two additional independent alarm types (e.g., fire alarm, water alarm) with their own sensor circuits. These operate independently from the main alarm system.
info_zones: Group sensors into up to three independent zones. Zones provide separate change detection — each zone reports its own state changes independently from the main alarm.
info_presence: Simulate presence by automatically toggling lights or switches based on time ranges, sunrise/sunset, or light sensor values. Each device can have its own schedule, duration, and randomization settings.
info_sayit_table: Select a text-to-speech state (e.g. sayit.0.tts.text) and choose which phrases to announce. Column mapping: 1 = Activated, 2 = Deactivated, x = Aborted, 3 = Activation failed, 4 = Warnings, 5 = Changes, 6 = Alarm/Burgle, 7 = Night rest begins, 8 = Night rest ends, 9 = Changes during night rest, 9+ = Sharp inside activated, 0 = Sharp inside deactivated, time = Countdown, one = Alarm type 1 (Fire), two = Alarm type 2 (Water).
